scene: A square, edge-to-edge {{material}} game tile featuring a deeply carved {{centerpiece}} as the central focal relief, flanked by {{flanking}} motifs, with {{filler}} details forming a decorative border and negative-space filler engravings.

subjects.description: deeply carved, bold silhouette with {{inlay}}-lined engraved accents and clean readable contours
subjects.interaction: dominant focal symbol; all other motifs frame it

subjects.description: two smaller symmetrical panels with simplified, readable details and subtle {{inlay}}-lined detailing
subjects.interaction: balances the central motif; provides thematic context

subjects.description: repeating decorative shapes carved as a band near the top and bottom regions
subjects.interaction: frames the tile and reinforces cultural theme

subjects.description: small reliefs and micro-engraved border ornaments filling remaining surface as decorative border relief
subjects.position: around edges and remaining negative space
subjects.interaction: fills remaining surface while preserving readability of main icon

style: hand-painted cel-shaded game art; flat color fills with subtle bevel lighting; crisp icon readability
lighting: stylized top-left key light; gentle bevel highlights on upper-left edges; deeper shadows cast toward bottom-right within carved grooves; {{inlay}} inlay catches slightly brighter highlights
mood: ancient, bold, mystical, game-readable, premium artifact

background.details: no separate background layer; the entire canvas is the tile surface. All visible pixels belong to the {{material}} tile; no white border, no transparency, no backdrop.

composition: center focus with symmetrical flanking panels; {{centerpiece}} occupies ~60% of the square; decorative bands and border relief fill remaining space; designed for clean readability at 48px

camera.focus: uniform sharp focus across full tile; crisp edges and outlines

medium: digital hand-painted illustration simulating carved {{material}} relief with {{inlay}}-lined inlay
resolution: 1024x1024 square (tile-asset ready, scales down cleanly to 48px)
details: 2-3 tonal variations of the same hue family for carved depth (lighter highlights and deeper recess shadows)
thin black outlines (2-3px equivalent) separating major shapes and element boundaries
flat fills with subtle bevel shading: lighter top-left, darker bottom-right
{{inlay}}-lined edges and details on the {{centerpiece}} and select engravings
clean, bold, simplified shapes for legibility at small sizes
tile fills the canvas edge-to-edge with no padding
effects: edge-integrated surface vignette (tile-only shading, not background)
subtle ambient occlusion in deeper carvings
minimal specular glint on {{inlay}} inlay (controlled, not glossy)
usage_notes: Treat this as a single opaque tile asset: the canvas is fully covered by the {{material}} tile. Apply the vignette as a surface shading pass on the {{material}} (multiply/darken of the {{material}} color), not as a background behind the tile. Preserve strong silhouettes and crisp 2-3px outlines for readability at 48px. Keep gradients minimal - only subtle bevel lighting and the edge shading.

tile_vignette.region: outermost 8-10% of the tile perimeter on all sides
tile_vignette.behavior: smooth, seamless darkening applied to the {{material}} texture itself (fully opaque), increasing toward the extreme edges
tile_vignette.color_method: darken/multiply the base {{material}} tones toward near-black at the extreme edge without introducing a separate black background layer
tile_vignette.notes: no visible background; no white or transparent margins; the vignette must not read as a backdrop

generation_hints.line_weight: 2-3px equivalent black outline between major shapes
generation_hints.edge_rule: the darkest pixels occur at the extreme perimeter but remain part of the {{material}} surface, not an external background
